title: In-Application Preview of Uploaded PDF Documents
description: The system shall provide an in-application preview of uploaded PDF documents. Users shall view PDF pages, navigate pages, zoom, and search within the document without leaving the application.
threeStageFlow: Upload PDF → Document Validation → Document Preview & Extraction → AI Extraction → Extraction Review → User Approve/Reject → Commit to Requirements Workspace → Requirements Studio
keyDistinction: AI Extraction ≠ Requirement Commitment. Human must explicitly commit approved requirements to the Requirements Workspace.

reviewWorkspaceLayout.panel1: PDF Preview — original document pages with page navigation, zoom, search, and source highlighting
reviewWorkspaceLayout.panel2: Extracted Content — text, tables, sections extracted from PDF
reviewWorkspaceLayout.panel3: AI Findings — requirements, confidence, source citations, Approve/Reject actions

title: Document Processing Status During Validation, Extraction, and AI Analysis
description: The system shall display document processing status during validation, extraction, and AI analysis using a defined lifecycle.

title: Display Extracted Text and Structured Content for User Review Before Requirements Are Committed
description: The system shall display extracted text and structured content for user review before requirements are committed. Users shall be able to toggle between Original PDF, Extracted Text, and Structured Content views.
reviewStages: Stage 1 — Extraction Review: Did AI correctly understand the document? (text, tables, sections, source locations, OCR quality)
Stage 2 — Requirements Review: Did AI correctly interpret business requirements? (wording, rules, eligibility logic, conflicts, confidence, traceability)

title: Display Extracted Tables in Structured Format
description: The system shall display extracted tables in a structured format where table extraction is detected. Users shall be able to mark table extraction as correct or flag for correction.

title: Display AI-Generated Requirements Separately from Extracted Content
description: The system shall display AI-generated requirements separately from the original extracted content in a distinct panel. The distinction between raw extracted text and AI-interpreted requirements shall be visually clear.

title: Each AI-Generated Requirement Includes Source Document Reference and Page Citation
description: Each AI-generated requirement shall include a source document reference and page-level citation where available. The citation shall be displayed on the requirement review card.

requirementReviewCard.title: Full-Time Employee Eligibility
requirementReviewCard.text: Employees working 30+ hours per week are eligible for medical coverage.

title: Navigate from AI-Generated Requirement to Corresponding Source Page
description: Users shall be able to navigate from an AI-generated requirement to the corresponding source page in the original document. Clicking [View Source] shall navigate the PDF preview to the relevant page.

title: Highlight Source Text in PDF Associated with AI-Generated Requirement
description: The system shall highlight the source text associated with an AI-generated requirement where technically feasible. Source highlighting is a key demo differentiator for AI traceability.
highlightFlow: Requirement → Source Citation → PDF Page → Highlighted Text
note: Implement where PDF text layer enables it. Fallback: scroll to page without highlight if text layer is unavailable.

title: Approve, Edit, or Reject AI-Generated Requirements Before Committing to Requirements Workspace
description: Users shall be able to approve, edit, or reject AI-generated requirements before committing them to the Requirements Workspace. Status values per finding: APPROVED, EDITED, REJECTED, PENDING_REVIEW.

title: Select Individual Requirements for Commitment to Requirements Workspace
description: The system shall allow users to select individual requirements for commitment. A bulk-select and [Commit N Requirements] action shall be provided.

commitConfirmation.message: 4 requirements will be added to Acme Corp 2027 Benefits Requirements Workspace and become available for review and downstream Plan Configuration / Rule Builder actions.

title: No Automatic Commitment of AI-Generated Requirements Without Explicit User Approval
description: The system shall not automatically commit AI-generated requirements to the Requirements Workspace without explicit user approval. Human review is always required between AI extraction and requirement commitment.
architecturePrinciple: AI Requirements Engineering positions AI as an accelerator for human experts, not as an autonomous system. Auto-commitment would undermine the trust and governance story of the demo.

title: Preserve Original Extracted Document Content Independently from Committed Requirements
description: The system shall preserve the original extracted document content independently from committed requirements. The finding record (pre-commitment) and the requirement record (post-commitment) are distinct data objects.

title: Traceability Between Source Document, Extracted Finding, Committed Requirement, and Downstream Artifacts
description: The system shall maintain traceability between the source document, extracted finding, committed requirement, and downstream artifacts (rules, plan configurations, test cases).

title: Configurable Confidence Thresholds for Prioritizing AI-Generated Requirements for Human Review
description: The system shall support configurable confidence thresholds for prioritizing AI-generated requirements for human review.
thresholdNote: Confidence is a review prioritization signal only — not a correctness guarantee. Thresholds are configurable.
